pattern: The forced replacement splits into two clean cases: either spend it on an unused element, or burn one slot for an extreme value paired with the two strongest survivors.
hints: The new value only helps if it is as extreme as allowed, so think of it as plus or minus the maximum magnitude.
Either the replaced element is not one of your three (only possible when there are spare elements), or it is, in which case it multiplies your best surviving pair.
Compare the ordinary best product of three (when n exceeds three) against the largest-magnitude pair times the extreme replacement value.

edge_inputs.description: replace a zero with an extreme
spare element lets best three stand
two large negatives pair with extreme

stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = max(3, min(size, 2000))
    nums = [rng.randrange(-100000, 100001) for _ in range(n)]
    return [nums]

stress.description: small arrays near the size-three boundary
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = rng.randrange(3, 7)
    nums = [rng.randrange(-100000, 100001) for _ in range(n)]
    return [nums]

solutions.python:
class Solution:
    def maxProduct(self, nums):
        n = len(nums)
        R = 100000
        arr = sorted(nums)
        best = None
        if n >= 4:
            p1 = arr[-1] * arr[-2] * arr[-3]
            p2 = arr[0] * arr[1] * arr[-1]
            best = max(p1, p2)
        pair_max = max(arr[-1] * arr[-2], arr[0] * arr[1], arr[0] * arr[-1])
        pair_min = min(arr[-1] * arr[-2], arr[0] * arr[1], arr[0] * arr[-1])
        case2 = max(pair_max, -pair_min) * R
        if best is None or case2 > best:
            best = case2
        return best
